meta.title: Prolonged infection of human synovial cells with Ross River virus -- Journeaux et al. 68 (12): 3165 -- Journal of General Virology

abstractHtml: Primary cultures of human synovial cells shed infectious virus for 14 to 35 days following infection with isolates of Ross River virus which had been passaged in the C6/36 line of Aedes albopictus mosquito cells. No frank cytopathic effect was seen in infected synovial cells and they continued to replicate for the duration of the experiments.
